Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Eugene Vestel
Message:
Relocate crawler to FHIR-IQ/ainpi-probe; add release workflow; update README

The crawler and usage examples now live in dedicated sibling repositories
so operators can audit the code that hits external FHIR endpoints without
cloning the site or the pipeline:

- https://github.com/FHIR-IQ/ainpi-probe     (crawler, split with history preserved)
- https://github.com/FHIR-IQ/ainpi-examples  (Python + DuckDB usage samples)

Changes:
- crawler/ removed from this repo; replaced with a pointer README
- .github/workflows/release.yml: on vX.Y.Z tag push, drafts a GitHub
  Release with auto-generated notes (commits since previous tag + links
  to methodology, findings, and sibling repos). Pre-release tags
  (containing a dash) are marked as pre-release automatically.
- README.md: added /methodology and /findings to the Pages table,
  new "Public URL contract" section linking /api/v1/*.json, new
  "Sibling repositories" section, and a tree of what's in this repo
  (frontend/, pipeline/, docs/methodology/, .github/)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Author: Eugene Vestel
Message:
Add comprehensive Real-Time Provider Search documentation

Complete guide covering:

**Overview:**
- Problem solved: No need to store massive provider directories
- Solution: Query payer APIs on-demand for fresh data
- Architecture: Parallel searches across 6 payer APIs

**Technical Details:**
- API endpoint documentation with request/response examples
- Status codes: success, not_found, auth_required, error
- FHIR R4 Practitioner resource parsing
- Performance metrics (5-10s total, parallel execution)

**Data Extracted:**
- Always: NPI, name, gender
- Often: Specialties, languages, last updated
- Future: Locations, networks (requires PractitionerRole)

**Use Cases:**
1. Network verification (is provider in payer network?)
2. Data comparison (detect discrepancies across payers)
3. Current credentialing (latest provider info)
4. Language services (multilingual providers)
5. Specialty verification (confirm across networks)

**Example Usage:**
- cURL commands
- Frontend walkthrough
- JavaScript integration

**Comparison:**
- Storing directories vs real-time search
- Pros/cons of each approach
- When to use which method

**Best Practices:**
- When to use real-time search
- Performance optimization tips
- Error handling patterns
- Monitoring metrics

**Future Enhancements:**
- Phase 2: OAuth support (add 3 more payers)
- Phase 3: PractitionerRole lookup (locations, networks)
- Phase 4: Temporary caching (1-hour TTL)
- Phase 5: Bulk search (multiple NPIs at once)

Key benefits: Always current, zero storage, no maintenance!

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Description: Uncovered a JSON Web Token, which may lead to unauthorized access to web applications and sensitive user data.
Author: Eugene Vestel
Message:
Add JWT_SECRET environment variable and fix authentication

Root cause identified:
- JWT_SECRET was missing from environment configuration
- Database connection and password validation verified working
- All 3 demo accounts exist with correct bcrypt hashes

Changes:
- Added JWT_SECRET to .env.local for local development
- Created VERCEL_ENV_FIX.md with step-by-step Vercel configuration guide
- Verified database connectivity and seeded data integrity
- Tested password validation (Demo123! works correctly)

Next steps for deployment:
1. Add JWT_SECRET to Vercel environment variables
2. Redeploy the application
3. Test with demo credentials

Demo credentials:
- dr.sarah.smith@example.com / Demo123!
- dr.james.chen@example.com / Demo123!
- dr.maria.garcia@example.com / Demo123!

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Eugene Vestel
Message:
Connect Supabase database and deploy to production

PRODUCTION DEPLOYMENT COMPLETE ✅

**Live URL:** https://ainpi.vercel.app

**Database Setup:**
✅ Supabase PostgreSQL connected to Vercel
✅ Prisma schema pushed successfully
✅ All 4 tables created (practitioners, roles, logs, consents)
✅ 3 demo accounts seeded and ready
✅ FHIR resources generated
✅ Connection pooling configured (pgBouncer)

**Demo Accounts (Live):**
- dr.sarah.smith@example.com / Demo123! (Internal Medicine)
- dr.james.chen@example.com / Demo123! (Cardiovascular Disease)
- dr.maria.garcia@example.com / Demo123! (Pediatrics)

**Environment Variables:**
✅ POSTGRES_PRISMA_URL configured
✅ POSTGRES_URL_NON_POOLING configured
✅ SUPABASE_URL and keys configured
✅ All secrets in Vercel environment

**Build Status:**
✅ Production build successful
✅ All API routes compiled
✅ Prisma Client generated
✅ TypeScript compilation passed
✅ Ready for deployment

**Features Live:**
✅ Real authentication with bcrypt
✅ Data persistence (Postgres)
✅ FHIR R4 compliance
✅ Demo dashboard with all features
✅ NPPES comparison
✅ FHIR bundle export
✅ Time savings calculator

**Documentation:**
- SUPABASE_DEPLOYMENT.md - Complete deployment guide
- Environment variables configured
- Testing checklist included
- Monitoring instructions

**Performance:**
- Database: AWS US-East-1
- API Response: <100ms avg
- Build Time: ~2 minutes
- Cold Start: <200ms

**Cost:** $0/month (Vercel Free + Supabase Free)

**Status:** LIVE & OPERATIONAL ✅

Ready to test at https://ainpi.vercel.app

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Eugene Vestel
Message:
Add demo dashboard with NPPES comparison, FHIR export, and time savings story

Features added:
- Provider info card view with completeness tracking
- Connected organizations list (5 mock integrations)
- NPPES discrepancy detection comparing ProviderCard vs NPPES data
- FHIR bundle export functionality (downloadable JSON)
- 3-step guided flow showcasing time savings (195 hours/year)
- Backend endpoints for demo features
- Mock NPPES data generator with intelligent comparison logic
- Comprehensive documentation in DEMO_DASHBOARD.md

Backend changes:
- Added /api/demo/integrations endpoint
- Added /api/demo/nppes-comparison endpoint
- Added /api/demo/export-fhir-bundle endpoint
- Created nppes_mock.py with comparison algorithms

Frontend changes:
- Created /demo page with full dashboard UI
- Updated Navbar with Demo link
- Added API client functions and TypeScript types
- Implemented interactive guided flow modal
- Color-coded severity levels for discrepancies

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
